purpose: Artifact contracts for deterministic attribute report tables generated from retailer source packages.

execution_contract.deterministic_boundary: The table builder reads package CSVs, selects rows, formats values, writes CSV/HTML artifacts, and returns a manifest. The model chooses the table and interprets the rows, but does not compute or render the table.

capabilities.description: Compare selected attribute bundles across focus and baseline cohorts.
capabilities.use_when: Use when the report needs exact bundle support for winning-now or emerging-signal rows, including focus/baseline counts, shares, delta, index, and brand breadth.
capabilities.avoid_when: Do not use for shelf visibility decomposition or individual product validation; use the visibility or product table instead.

capabilities.description: Show whether selected bundles appear as current winners, emerging recent-product signals, or both.
capabilities.use_when: Use when the report needs to compare current winners with emerging signals and explicitly show alignment, divergence, or one-sided support.
capabilities.avoid_when: Do not use when only one cohort layer exists or when the question is about product examples rather than bundle alignment.

capabilities.description: Present selected web-shelf lanes with gross, incremental, cumulative, and robustness metrics.
capabilities.use_when: Use when the report needs to ground claims about rank-weighted shelf visibility, incremental lane contribution, or robustness across alpha assumptions.
capabilities.avoid_when: Do not use as sales, demand, or causality support; use it only as shelf-visibility support.

capabilities.description: Link representative products to selected attribute signals with rank, review, image, and caveat fields.
capabilities.use_when: Use when the report needs product-level grounding for selected bundles, standout examples, or PDP/review reality checks; prefer image cards in the report narrative and this table as compact validation detail.
capabilities.avoid_when: Do not use to rank the category as a whole or to compare bundle prevalence; use the bundle comparison table for that.
